summary: Soundboard source with occasional drop‑outs captures a soaring second set highlighted by Franklin’s Tower, Jack‑a‑Roe and a vulnerable Garcia.
review: The show is praised as one of the best of ’78, especially the free‑form jams of the second set – half‑step (The Other One), Franklin’s Tower and a freight‑train‑like “Jack‑a‑Roe” are called out as standout moments.  The SBD recording is generally good but suffers from some late‑set board drop‑outs, likely inherited from the original cassette source.

ai_rating.rationale: Three solid positive reviews (4–5 stars) highlight a strong performance and only minor recording flaws; rating reflects high show quality with good but not flawless sound.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall clear SBD capture, but reviewers note occasional board drop‑outs toward the end of the set; original cassette source shows age.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑set jamming soars despite Bob’s illness; free‑form, studio‑feel improvisations dominate and keep the momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as “vulnerable” on WTG, indicating a deeply emotive guitar/vocal performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob was reportedly sick and missed the start of the second set, affecting the band’s lineup for that portion.
